comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Two failures of `[It] podman create pod with id file with network portbindings` on two different remote root test suites... Could be a flake (and restarted in case it is) but makes me suspicious.

/lgtm
(In case it is just a flake)

> Two failures of [It] podman create pod with id file with network portbindings on two different remote root test suites... Could be a flake (and restarted in case it is) but makes me suspicious.

Ah no that is a test I changed, even if they are flakes this would likely introduce a new one. I guess I have to take a closer look when remote does, by the time run -d completes the port should always be bound, weird.

/hold cancel

Ok the retry loop is needed because we make a connection to the container process and that of course might not have bound the port so quickly.
